instruction: What are the steps to using random (yet coherent) text as filler content within your Word document?
output:
Steps to using random (yet coherent) text as filler content within your Word document:
1. Open a new Word document.
2. Click the "Insert" tab on the Ribbon.
3. Click the "Object" tab.
4. Click the "Text" button.
5. In the "Text" box that appears, type the following code:

```
<w:r><w:t>Your desired filler text goes here.</w:t></w:r>
```
6. Select the text you just added and choose a font, font size, and font color from the formatting options that appear.
7. Click the "OK" button when you are finished.
8. Your filler text should appear in the document.

instruction:
You should take the time to read the situation and problem carefully and give the best advice you can.

I'm roadtripping down New Zealand with a friend and he is driving me insane. He hasn't enjoyed all the tours we've been on and doesn't want to chat about ANYTHING. We're a week into our trip and this is the quietest I've ever been when hanging out with another person as he rebuffs all my attempts at conversation. He wears his airpods in the car and around the accommodation - not that I've kept trying to talk to him. Honestly, he is bringing some seriously bad vibes to everything and it's ruining my trip.
We don't have anything booked in yet for the South Island, so I'm planning to ditch him in Wellington at the ferry. Probably book my own car in Picton and just relax alone for the rest of the trip. (Just thinking about it is such sharp relief that it makes me feel emotional. I'll pay whatever price I have to just to get away from him.)
Obviously telling him he is ruining my trip with his negative energy isn't the best way to approach this.
Any advice for the best approach so we can split amicably? Or at least not have an argument that ends with very hurt feelings?
output: It sounds like you have already made the decision to part ways, so I would suggest being kind and gentle in your approach. You don't have to be mean or cruel, but you could try saying something like, "I'm really enjoying my trip, and I'd like to do things my way. Would you be okay with that?" or "I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed by the conversations we've been having, so I'd like to take some time to myself. Is that okay?" Being honest and direct can help you avoid an argument, and it will show that you care about his feelings. Good luck!

instruction: Write a step-by-step guide for resolving GitHub merge conflicts.
output:
1. Open the Merge Conflicts tab in GitHub.
2. Identify the conflict by looking at the differences between the versions.
3. Select the files that have conflicts and add them to the Merge Conflicts tab.
4. Create a temporary local branch to work on the conflicts.
5. Edit the files to resolve the conflicts.
6. Commit the changes to the local branch.
7. Run the command "git diff" to get the hash of the commit.
8. Edit the Merge Conflicts tab and select the "Resolve with Squash Merge" option.
9. Enter the commit hash from step 6 to the "Squash Merge" field.
10. Select the "Merge" button to merge the changes.
11. Check the Merge Conflicts tab to make sure the conflicts are resolved.
12. Run the "git push" command to push the changes to the remote repository.

Mathematics and art have a long historical relationship. Artists have used mathematics since the 4th century BC when the Greek sculptor Polykleitos wrote his Canon, prescribing proportions conjectured to have been based on the ratio 1:√2 for the ideal male nude. Persistent popular claims have been made for the use of the golden ratio in ancient art and architecture, without reliable evidence. In the Italian Renaissance, Luca Pacioli wrote the influential treatise De divina proportione (1509), illustrated with woodcuts by Leonardo da Vinci, on the use of the golden ratio in art. Another Italian painter, Piero della Francesca, developed Euclid's ideas on perspective in treatises such as De Prospectiva Pingendi, and in his paintings. The engraver Albrecht Dürer made many references to mathematics in his work Melencolia I. In modern times, the graphic artist M. C. Escher made intensive use of tessellation and hyperbolic geometry, with the help of the mathematician H. S. M. Coxeter, while the De Stijl movement led by Theo van Doesburg and Piet Mondrian explicitly embraced geometrical forms. Mathematics has inspired textile arts such as quilting, knitting, cross-stitch, crochet, embroidery, weaving, Turkish and other carpet-making, as well as kilim. In Islamic art, symmetries are evident in forms as varied as Persian girih and Moroccan zellige tilework, Mughal jali pierced stone screens, and widespread muqarnas vaulting.

instruction:

The "fair trade" movement, also known as the "trade justice" movement, promotes the use of labour, environmental and social standards for the production of commodities, particularly those exported from the Third and Second Worlds to the First World. Such ideas have also sparked a debate on whether trade itself should be codified as a human right.
output:
The "fair trade" movement, also known as the "trade justice" movement, promotes the use of labour, environmental and social standards for the production of commodities, particularly those exported from the Third, Second and First Worlds to the First World. Such ideas have also sparked a debate on whether trade itself should be codified as a human right. 

The Fair Trade movement seeks to ensure that producers in developing countries are provided with better working conditions and more equitable trading relationships. This movement has grown rapidly in recent years and is now seen as an influential force in global commerce. Supporters of the Fair Trade movement argue that it helps to lift producers out of poverty, by providing them with higher wages and better working conditions. They also argue that it helps to promote sustainable development, by ensuring that production is carried out in an ethical and responsible manner.

Fair Trade is not just limited to the production of goods, it also encompasses the trading of services. For example, in the United Kingdom, there are now a number of Fair Trade law firms that provide free or reduced cost legal advice to those in need. This helps to ensure that anyone, regardless of their income, can access justice. 

Fair Trade also seeks to help disadvantaged communities in the developing world gain access to global markets. This is done by providing training, advice and support to these communities so that they can set up their own businesses and trade their products. This helps to create sustainable livelihoods and break the cycle of poverty. 

In conclusion, the Fair Trade movement is a powerful force in global commerce and is helping to lift producers in the developing world out of poverty. By providing better working conditions and trading relationships, it is ensuring that producers are treated ethically and responsibly, whilst also providing access to global markets for disadvantaged communities. It is clear that Fair Trade has the potential to make a real difference in the lives of producers in the developing world, and it is up to all of us to ensure that it succeeds.

instruction:
Describe how to prepare the given food in your own words. Note down the ingredients you will need and the steps you will take to prepare them.

Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies
output:
To make Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies, you will need the following ingredients: 2 cups of all-purpose flour, 1 teaspoon of baking soda, 1 teaspoon of salt, ¾ cup of butter, ¾ cup of granulated sugar, ¾ cup of packed brown sugar, 2 large eggs, 2 teaspoons of vanilla extract, 60-70% dark chocolate chips, and optional ingredients such as pecans or walnuts. 

To begin, preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Next, mix the dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, and salt) together in a medium bowl. In a larger bowl, cream together the butter and sugars until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add the vanilla extract. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until combined. Finally, fold in the chocolate chips and optional nuts, if using. 

Scoop the cookie dough onto an ungreased baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes. Allow the cookies to cool for 5 minutes before transferring to a cooling rack. Enjoy!

instruction: Suggest a recipe for a vegan dessert.
output:
This vegan dessert recipe is sure to delight! Start by preheating your oven to 350 degrees. In a medium bowl, combine 1 cup of rolled oats, 1/2 cup of almond milk, 1/4 cup of vegan butter, 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Mix until everything is well combined. Grease a 9-inch pie dish with vegan butter. Press the oat mixture into the dish, and set aside.

In a separate bowl, combine 1/2 cup of all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup of rolled oats, 1/2 cup of vegan butter, 1/2 cup of almond milk, 1/4 cup of vegan dark chocolate chips, 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Mix until everything is well combined. Spread the mixture evenly over the oat crust. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown. Allow to cool before serving. Enjoy!

instruction: Design a medium-level sudoku puzzle.
output:
A medium-level sudoku puzzle contains 81 blocks and 81 cells. The top left corner of the puzzle contains the numbers 1 to 9, indicating the rows and columns of the puzzle. The remaining cells contain the numbers 1 to 9, indicating the various boxes of the puzzle. 

The goal of the puzzle is to fill in all the boxes with the numbers 1 to 9 so that each row, column, and 3x3 box contain the numbers 1 to 9 only once.

In order to solve the puzzle, various strategies can be used. One strategy is to look for the number 1 in the top right corner and place it in the first row and column. This will indicate the first row and column of the puzzle. Then the number 2 can be found in the top right corner and placed in the second row and column. This process can be repeated until all the numbers 1 to 9 are placed in the correct boxes.

instruction:
Give a brief explanation of the requested part of the paper.

Paper: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train. Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles, by over 2 BLEU. On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature. We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.
Explain: The dominant sequence transduction models
output: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. This mechanism allows the model to focus on certain parts of the input while discarding others, creating shorter and more efficient representations of the input. This network architecture, called the Transformer, dispenses with recurrence and convolutions entirely, relying solely on attention mechanisms. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train. On the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, the Transformer achieves a BLEU score of 28.4, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles, by over 2 BLEU. On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, the Transformer establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature. The Transformer also generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.

This task presents specific challenges: the output is subject to strong structural constraints and is significantly longer than the input. Question: What is English constituency parsing?
output: English constituency parsing is a task that requires a machine to analyze the syntactic structure of a sentence and to assign each word in the sentence to a part of speech tag, part of speech, or word class. It is a challenging task as the output is subject to strong structural constraints and is significantly longer than the input. RNN sequence-to-sequence models have not been able to attain state-of-the-art results in small-data regimes.

instruction:

We present a state-of-the-art speech recognition system developed using end-to-end deep learning. Our architecture is significantly simpler than traditional speech systems, which rely on laboriously engineered processing pipelines; these traditional systems also tend to perform poorly when used in noisy environments. In contrast, our system does not need hand-designed components to model background noise, reverberation, or speaker variation, but instead directly learns a function that is robust to such effects. We do not need a phoneme dictionary, nor even the concept of a "phoneme." Key to our approach is a well-optimized RNN training system that uses multiple GPUs, as well as a set of novel data synthesis techniques that allow us to efficiently obtain a large amount of varied data for training. Our system, called Deep Speech, outperforms previously published results on the widely studied Switchboard Hub5'00, achieving 16.0% error on the full test set. Deep Speech also handles challenging noisy environments better than widely used, state-of-the-art commercial speech systems.Collapse

instruction:
Create a short, concise summary of the paper based on its abstract.

Few-shot learning (FSL) is one of the key future steps in machine learning and raises a lot of attention. In this paper, we focus on the FSL problem of dialogue understanding, which contains two closely related tasks: intent detection and slot filling. Dialogue understanding has been proven to benefit a lot from jointly learning the two sub-tasks. However, such joint learning becomes challenging in the few-shot scenarios: on the one hand, the sparsity of samples greatly magnifies the difficulty of modeling the connection between the two tasks; on the other hand, how to jointly learn multiple tasks in the few-shot setting is still less investigated. In response to this, we introduce FewJoint, the first FSL benchmark for joint dialogue understanding. FewJoint provides a new corpus with 59 different dialogue domains from real industrial API and a code platform to ease FSL experiment set-up, which are expected to advance the research of this field. Further, we find that insufficient performance of the few-shot setting often leads to noisy sharing between two sub-task and disturbs joint learning. To tackle this, we guide slot with explicit intent information and propose a novel trust gating mechanism that blocks low-confidence intent information to ensure high quality sharing. Besides, we introduce a Reptile-based meta-learning strategy to achieve better generalization in unseen few-shot domains. In the experiments, the proposed method brings significant improvements on two datasets and achieve new state-of-the-art performance.
output: This paper introduces FewJoint, a new benchmark for few-shot learning in dialogue understanding, which provides a new corpus and a code platform to ease the set-up of FSL experiments. It also finds that insufficient performance of the few-shot setting often leads to noisy sharing between two sub-tasks and disturbs joint learning. To tackle this, the paper guides slot filling with explicit intent information and introduces a Reptile-based meta-learning strategy. The experiments bring significant improvements on two datasets and achieve a new state-of-the-art performance.

instruction: Create a template in markdown to create scope for Jira tickets. Members should use this template as a checklist to ensure they have included all the necessary information when creating a ticket.

### Jira Ticket Scope Template

### Summary:

The ticket's summary should provide a concise description of the issue, relevant to the project. This should include a brief description of the issue and the expected outcome.

### Description:

The description should provide a detailed explanation of the issue and any background information necessary to understand the issue. This should include a description of the symptoms and the cause.

### Type:

The ticket type should designate the type of issue being reported. This should include information such as whether the issue is a bug, feature request, or another type.

### Environment:

The environment should provide information about the environment in which the issue is occurring. This should include information such as the operating system, application version, or other details.

### Steps to Reproduce:

The ticket should also include instructions on how to reproduce the issue. This should include details on what actions to take to cause the issue.

### Expected Outcome:

The expected outcome should provide an expectation of the outcome of the issue. This should include the outcome that the user expects as a result of the issue being resolved.

### Attachments:

If relevant, the ticket can include any relevant attachments such as screenshots or other documents. This should include any attachments that can help the team understand the issue.

### Verified:

The ticket should also include information about whether the issue has been verified or not. This should provide an indication of whether the reporter has verified the issue or not.

instruction: For a quick and efficient office workout, suggest a short routine.
output:
A quick and efficient office workout routine could include: 
1. High knees - stand up, take one step forward with each knee lift 
2. Push-ups - place hands on desk, do push-ups, then rest your body on the desk 
3. Squats - stand up, sit down, repeat 
4. Plank - hold for one minute 
5. Mountain Climbers - place hands on the desk, alternate legs in climbing motion 
6. Bicycle - place hands on desk, alternate legs in pedaling motion 
7. Shoulder Press - hold a light dumbbell in each hand, press outward 
8. Tricep Dips - start in a sitting position, then lower down to the floor for one minute 
9. Chair Lunges - stand with feet hip width apart, lunge forward 
10. Squat Jumps - start in a squat position, jump up as high as you can

instruction:
Using the ingredients that are available, create a dinner recipe.

Kale + almond butter + apple cider vinegar + agave nectar + tamari
output:
Kale Salad with Apple Cider Vinegar Vinaigrette: 
Ingredients: 2 cups kale, 1/2 cup almond butter, 2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar, 2 tablespoons agave nectar, 2 tablespoons tamari. 
Instructions:
1. Put the kale in a large bowl.
2. Spread the almond butter on the kale.
3. Drizzle the apple cider vinegar, agave nectar and tamari over the kale and mix together.
4. Put the kale salad in the fridge for 10 minutes before serving.
